title: Fix: useEffect cleanup runs twice in StrictMode (#30000)
body: When using React with concurrent features enabled, I noticed that the component re-renders unexpectedly when state is updated from useEffect cleanup. Steps to reproduce: 1) Create a new React app, 2) Add the component described above, 3) Interact with the UI. Expected behavior: Component should render correctly. Actual behavior: See description above. React version: 19.0.0. Browser: Chrome 120

title: Bug: Hydration mismatch with Suspense and streaming SSR (#29999)
body: When using React with concurrent features enabled, I noticed that useTransition causes a brief flash of loading state even when the transition completes synchronously. Steps to reproduce: 1) Create a new React app, 2) Add the component described above, 3) Interact with the UI. Expected behavior: Component should render correctly. Actual behavior: See description above. React version: 18.3.1. Browser: Chrome 121

title: Feature: Add support for async server actions in forms (#29998)
body: When using React with concurrent features enabled, I noticed that Suspense boundaries sometimes show fallback unnecessarily when navigating between cached routes. Steps to reproduce: 1) Create a new React app, 2) Add the component described above, 3) Interact with the UI. Expected behavior: Component should render correctly. Actual behavior: See description above. React version: 19.1.0-canary. Browser: Chrome 122

title: Performance: Reduce re-renders in concurrent mode transitions (#29997)
body: When using React with concurrent features enabled, I noticed that the ref callback fires twice in StrictMode even though the component only mounts once. Steps to reproduce: 1) Create a new React app, 2) Add the component described above, 3) Interact with the UI. Expected behavior: Component should render correctly. Actual behavior: See description above. React version: 19.0.0. Browser: Chrome 123

title: Bug: useCallback dependencies not tracked with destructuring (#29996)
body: When using React with concurrent features enabled, I noticed that act() warnings appear in tests when using React Testing Library with async state updates. Steps to reproduce: 1) Create a new React app, 2) Add the component described above, 3) Interact with the UI. Expected behavior: Component should render correctly. Actual behavior: See description above. React version: 18.3.1. Browser: Chrome 124

title: Enhancement: Better error messages for invalid hook calls (#29995)
body: When using React with concurrent features enabled, I noticed that memo() does not prevent re-renders when using context providers that change reference identity. Steps to reproduce: 1) Create a new React app, 2) Add the component described above, 3) Interact with the UI. Expected behavior: Component should render correctly. Actual behavior: See description above. React version: 19.1.0-canary. Browser: Chrome 120

title: Fix: Context value identity check causes unnecessary renders (#29994)
body: When using React with concurrent features enabled, I noticed that lazy() components cause hydration mismatches when used with streaming SSR. Steps to reproduce: 1) Create a new React app, 2) Add the component described above, 3) Interact with the UI. Expected behavior: Component should render correctly. Actual behavior: See description above. React version: 19.0.0. Browser: Chrome 121

title: Bug: lazy() SSR streaming produces hydration warnings (#29993)
body: When using React with concurrent features enabled, I noticed that useCallback dependencies are not properly tracked when using object destructuring in parameters. Steps to reproduce: 1) Create a new React app, 2) Add the component described above, 3) Interact with the UI. Expected behavior: Component should render correctly. Actual behavior: See description above. React version: 18.3.1. Browser: Chrome 122

title: Feature: useFormStatus should support nested form elements (#29992)
body: When using React with concurrent features enabled, I noticed that forwardRef components lose their displayName in React DevTools after upgrading to v19. Steps to reproduce: 1) Create a new React app, 2) Add the component described above, 3) Interact with the UI. Expected behavior: Component should render correctly. Actual behavior: See description above. React version: 19.1.0-canary. Browser: Chrome 123

title: Fix: act() warning in tests with async state updates (#29991)
body: When using React with concurrent features enabled, I noticed that the new use() hook throws an error when used with Promise.race() in a server component. Steps to reproduce: 1) Create a new React app, 2) Add the component described above, 3) Interact with the UI. Expected behavior: Component should render correctly.
